blocks.content: Armin reassured Shirone with a gentle smile.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: It's only natural. If you could control it from the beginning, you wouldn't have opened the Immortal Function. The surreal feeling you're experiencing is what you feel toward yourself. It's because you don't yet understand the enlightenment gained through that vast expansion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can I understand it?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: You'll realize it naturally. You just can't yet put a name to what you've already attained.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But…….
Seeing Shirone's uneasy expression, Armin was reminded of his own past. No matter how much someone told him he'd understand in time, it was hard to believe it would ever feel real.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Alright. Then I'll tell you. The name of what Shirone has realized is gauge symmetry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gauge…… symmetry?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: This world should be perfectly balanced. But in reality, symmetry is broken. You've directly experienced that dissonance. That's likely the cause of your surreal feeling.
Shirone repeated the term 'gauge symmetry' like a mantra. But nothing magical came to mind.
Armin patted Shirone's head and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: There's no need to worry about anything. It doesn't mean you should do nothing, but you'll eventually reach it. When that time comes, you'll understand what I mean.
Shirone took a deep breath and nodded. Though his mind was still unsettled, he knew today's meeting would become a major turning point in his future.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you so much. If I hadn't met you, Armin, I might have fallen into Overflow as Shiira-sensei warned.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Unlockers share a deep bond because they resonate on the same wavelength. Though sometimes that bond leads to big fights. Still, I'm glad I could help.
Armin smiled warmly at Shiira.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: I think it's time to leave the Room of Time now. I need to finish the painting before my wife returns.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, now that you mention it, you said today was the last day?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Yes. Though I have a grandiose title, my main profession is still a painter. I'll start packing today and leave in a few days. I won't be able to see Shiira for a while.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wouldn't it be better to talk to her directly……?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Shiira is still suffering. Even if I hadn't given up magic, her lost eye wouldn't grow back. Besides, I'm already married. I want to help her live her own life.
Shirone understood Armin's feelings. He didn't want to see his beloved younger sister trapped in the past.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright. I'll keep it a secret too. Thank you so much for today.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: I enjoyed it too, Shirone.
Armin covered his eyes with the cloth again. As he sat back at the canvas, time began to flow. The sounds of Nade and Iruki's breathing returned, and life returned to Shiira's smile.
Shirone felt as if he had awoken from a dream. It was hard to believe he had just been talking to Armin.
Time flew by in his heightened emotions. Armin finished the painting and turned the canvas toward Shiira.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: How is it? Does it look good?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Yeah, it's pretty.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Thanks for putting up with me. It was an honor to paint you.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Oppa. We're not strangers…….

A fleeting look of regret passed through Shiira's eyes. Then, hearing footsteps from downstairs, she startled up without even a chance to say goodbye. Was his wife already back? It was earlier than usual, but she didn't have the presence of mind to dwell on it.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Oppa. I'll get going now. You all should leave too.
Shiira tried to flee as if running away. Seeing her, who was always so dignified at school, cower just because Armin's wife was coming, made Shirone's group feel sour inside.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Darling, are you painting?
Keira peeked her head in. Contrary to expectations of someone as calm as Armin, she was a lively woman in her early twenties. Her expression turned startled when she met Shiira's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Oh my, Shiira? Now that I think about it, today was your day to visit.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Hello, Keira. It's been a while.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: It has. Why is it so hard to see you? Are you avoiding me on purpose? Hohoho!

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Ah, I'm done with Oppa's work. I'll be going now.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Since you're here, why the rush? Stay for dinner.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Thank you for the offer, but I have plans…….

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Hmm. That's suspicious. Darling, did you do something shady with Shiira while I was gone?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Keira, watch your words.
Armin's brow furrowed. Even Shirone thought it was uncalled for. The teacher had been careful with her demeanor from start to finish—how could she accuse her of something shady?
Shiira's face flushed red as she half-heartedly put on her coat and left the room.
Shirone's group, feeling upset, glared at Keira as they followed. But whether she was oblivious or just good-natured, Keira waved with a teasing smile.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Bye, kids. Next time you come, I'll make something delicious for you.
Of course, there was no response. Their cold attitude made Keira tilt her head and mutter.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Huh? Did I make a mistake? Why are they like that? Did you really do something weird here, darling?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Stop joking and come down. I want to say goodbye properly.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Tch. You're hopeless when it comes to your little sister.
Armin quickly descended the stairs. Shiira, feeling it wouldn't do to leave like this, waited outside the door.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Sorry about that, Shiira. My wife's always been a handful.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: It's fine. Anyway, if we part now, who knows when we'll meet again. Take care. Stay healthy.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Yeah. You too, work hard. Seeing you get along with your students puts my mind at ease. Honestly, I was worried if you'd do well. Hahaha!
Shiira let out a small laugh. Armin was probably the only person in the world who could treat her like a child. He was the only one who shared memories of her foolish younger days.

Shirone's chest tightened. He faintly understood why the kingdom's recognized genius had decided to become a teacher, why she was so obsessed with Overflow, and why she kept men at arm's length even after earning the title 'Frozen Shiira.'

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Then I'll be going. Write to me if you have time.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: And Shirone, Nade, Iruki. Do your best. I'll be listening from afar for news of your growth as great mages.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. Sorry for today.
Shirone's group expressed their sincere gratitude. Without Armin's consideration, things wouldn't have ended so smoothly.
Armin stood there until Shiira was out of sight. Keira, who had been leaning against the door with her arms crossed, spoke with a cold expression.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Armin, snap out of it. If the higher-ups find out you're lost in sentimentality, who knows what'll happen.
Armin also turned to Keira with a displeased expression. Though they had lived as a married couple for three years, he couldn't bring himself to like her.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Whatever feelings I have are my own. Just focus on your mission report. Is the investigation done?

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: Perfectly. We have no more business in Creas. We need to reach Calberra within 15 days. We'll pass through dangerous areas, but with Armin the Radiant, it shouldn't be a problem, right?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: No need for unnecessary chatter. I'll keep my promise.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: You didn't tell Shiira, did you?
Armin didn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Keira
blocks.content: I don't want to harm you. Of course, I know no method can trap the Eternal Contemplator, but you're still just human. The moment you betray the organization, everyone you love will experience hell. Shiira, even the Olipher faction.
It was a chilling threat—and the truth. The organization Keira belonged to had more than enough power, authority, and cruelty to make it happen.
No matter what, he had to protect Shiira. In that sense, meeting Shirone had been fortunate. Today's unexpected variable, which no one could have predicted, would someday turn the tide in his favor.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Don't worry.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Shiira knows nothing.
Shirone's group, returning to school, anxiously watched Shiira's demeanor.
While the mood had been fine with Armin, there was no telling when her feelings might change.
